response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web-systems engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is a poor match for this role across nearly every required dimension. Most critically, the role demands native-level Japanese proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement — and the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond language, the role is centered on embedded firmware for LAN-switch security features (MACsec, 802.1X, Linux kernel modification, chip SDKs, Layer-2/3 networking), while Bogdan's entire career is oriented toward web services, TypeScript, Node.js, and DevOps. His C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in his thesis is minimal and dated relative to what the role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple languages and platforms demonstrates strong engineering fundamentals
Some C and Linux exposure (NI LabWindows C/C++ components, Linux-based audio thesis project) provides a thin foundational touchpoint
Proven ability to work in complex, performance-sensitive environments (high-throughput micro-services at Sociomantic, embedded-adjacent hardware work at Altom)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated native-level hard requirement and is an immediate disqualifier
Zero demonstrated experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC auth), or Linux kernel development
Career trajectory is entirely web/cloud/TypeScript-focused with no signal of interest or background in embedded systems, network security, or switch chip development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or switch security experience
